metadata.title: Free Text to Speech Online
metadata.description: Convert text to natural speech and download MP3 instantly. Automatic language detection, 11 accents, 3 voice styles, and no registration required.
metadata.ogTitle: TTS Easy - Free Text to Speech Online
metadata.ogDescription: Create natural voice audio and download MP3 files in one click with automatic language detection and accent control.

home.h1: Free Text to Speech and MP3 Download - Convert Text to Audio Instantly
home.subtitle: Paste any text, generate natural voice audio, and download your MP3 in one click. No registration required.
home.compactH1: Free Text to Speech and MP3 Download
home.compactSubtitle: Paste text, generate natural speech, and download MP3 instantly.
editorialIntro: Text to speech is only useful when the path from draft to publish stays short, predictable, and easy to review. TTS Easy is built for people who need to move from raw copy to a usable MP3 without account friction, extra setup, or guesswork about language routing.

The public app focuses on practical production tasks: detect the likely language, surface the right accent variants, let you audition a small set of voice styles, and hand off an MP3 that is ready for video, lessons, demos, accessibility support, or internal review. We keep the workflow narrow on purpose so creators and small teams can publish faster.
home.featuredPostsDescription: Recent editorial guides that explain practical TTS workflows, tradeoffs, and implementation details.

ui.accentQuestion: Accent is unclear. Do you want to keep {primary} or switch to {secondary}?
ui.disclaimer: Text is processed in-memory only and is never stored.
ui.headline: Studio-grade text to speech
ui.subtitle: Generate natural voiceovers and download your MP3 in one click.
ui.mp3CalloutTitle: Generate and download MP3 in seconds
ui.mp3CalloutSubtitle: Turn any script into a polished MP3 file ready for videos, lessons, or social media.

items.title: Instant Language Detection
items.description: Language detection runs while you type and helps route scripts before you generate audio. That reduces avoidable voice mismatches on mixed-language or imported copy.

items.title: 11 Accents Supported
items.description: Accent variants are grouped around real publishing needs instead of hidden behind provider jargon. That makes it faster to move between US, UK, Australian, Spanish, and Portuguese output without re-learning the interface.

items.title: 3 Voice Styles
items.description: Natural, Clear, and Expressive modes give you a practical spread of voice behavior for tutorials, accessibility, and storytelling. The goal is fewer choices, but better defaults for common TTS jobs.

items.title: Free MP3 Download
items.description: Every successful run ends with an MP3 that is ready to hand off into editors, lessons, demos, and review loops. You do not need a second export tool just to get a usable file out.

items.title: No Registration Required
items.description: The web app is designed for immediate use, so you can validate scripts before you commit to a bigger workflow. That matters when you are testing formats, channels, or multilingual ideas quickly.

items.title: Privacy-First
items.description: Text is processed in memory, not turned into a content archive. The product is optimized for generation speed and review, not for collecting or reusing user text behind the scenes.

items.question: What is text to speech?
items.answer: Text to speech (TTS) is a technology that converts written text into spoken audio. TTS Easy uses advanced AI voices from Google Cloud to produce natural-sounding speech in multiple languages and accents.

items.question: Is TTS Easy free to use?
items.answer: Yes, TTS Easy is completely free. You can convert text to speech and download MP3 files without creating an account or paying anything.

items.question: What languages and accents are supported?
items.answer: TTS Easy supports 6 languages with 11 accent variants: English (US, UK, Australia), Spanish (Mexico, Spain, Argentina), Portuguese (Brazil, Portugal), French (France), German (Germany), and Italian (Italy).

items.question: Can I download the audio as MP3?
items.answer: Yes, every generated audio can be downloaded as an MP3 file with a single click. The file is ready for use in videos, presentations, or any other project.

items.question: Is my text stored or shared?
items.answer: No. Your text is processed in-memory only and is immediately discarded after the audio is generated. We never store, log, or share your text content.

items.question: How do I change the voice accent?
items.answer: TTS Easy automatically detects the language and accent of your text. You can also switch to Manual mode and select any of the 11 available accents from the dropdown menu.

items.question: Can I use the audio for YouTube videos?
items.answer: Yes, you can use the generated audio for YouTube videos, podcasts, presentations, e-learning content, and any other personal or commercial project.

about.title: About
about.p1: TTS Easy exists to make text to speech practical for everyday publishing work. Many TTS tools are technically capable but operationally noisy: too many setup steps, too many hidden controls, and too little clarity about what a creator or small team should do next. Our mission is to remove that drag. We want someone with a script, lesson, accessibility need, or product demo to move from plain text to a clean MP3 with as little friction as possible, while still understanding the tradeoffs behind the output.
about.p2: The core product is intentionally narrow. We focus on script input, language detection, accent routing, a compact set of voice styles, and fast MP3 delivery. Under the hood, the app connects those steps to cloud speech infrastructure, abuse protection, rate limits, analytics, and an API portal for prepaid usage. On the surface, the experience is meant to stay simple. We would rather help users complete a real workflow quickly than overwhelm them with low-signal controls that look impressive in a feature list but slow down production in practice.
about.p3: TTS Easy uses AI speech systems, and we are explicit about that. The app can accelerate script-to-audio work, but it does not replace editorial judgment, legal review, or accessibility testing. Generated voices still need human review when tone, factual accuracy, regional nuance, or brand risk matters. We also do not present translated or synthesized output as if it were automatically authoritative. The right use of AI here is operational leverage: faster drafts, quicker previews, and cleaner handoffs, with humans still responsible for final publishing decisions.
about.editorialPolicyBody: Our editorial process follows the same principle. We publish guides, comparisons, and workflow pages only when they help someone make a clearer decision or finish a concrete job. That means we try to state scope, intended use, limitations, and product fit plainly. We update pages when product behavior changes, when we can add evidence that makes the page more useful, or when a page starts to drift into thin templated copy. We do not treat the content layer as filler around ads; it should stand on its own as documentation and decision support.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.p1: We do not store the text you paste for speech generation. Your text is processed in-memory and discarded after audio is produced.
privacy.p2: We use analytics and advertising tools (Google Analytics and Google AdSense) to measure traffic and display ads. These tools may use cookies according to their own policies.
privacy.p3: Abuse prevention data (rate-limit counters and CAPTCHA verification status) is stored only as aggregated security metadata and never contains your raw text.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.p1: This service is provided on an as-is basis for converting text to speech. You agree not to use it for abusive, unlawful, or automated scraping behaviors.
terms.p2: We may enforce rate limits, CAPTCHA challenges, or temporary blocks to protect service availability and budget.
terms.p3: By using this website, you acknowledge that synthesized audio quality depends on selected locale, reader profile, and cloud provider capabilities.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.p1: This website uses cookies and similar technologies for three purposes: analytics (GA4), ad delivery (AdSense), and fraud prevention.
cookies.p2: You can control cookies from your browser settings. Blocking cookies may impact ad rendering and some personalization features.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.description: Choose the right text-to-speech workflow based on your goal, content format, and publishing rhythm.

useCases.howToChooseTitle: How to choose a use case
howToChooseItems: Start with your publishing channel: short-form video, long-form audio, learning content, or support material.
Match voice style and speed to audience expectations before final export.
Use one repeatable script structure so each new audio stays consistent.
whenToUseItems: When you need to move from raw script to MP3 quickly.
When you publish in multiple languages and want fewer manual steps.
When you need a focused workflow instead of a general feature list.
useCases.navigationDescription: After selecting a use case, continue with tools and comparisons to refine your production workflow.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Use supporting tools to clean scripts and validate language before generating final narration.
tools.metaDescription: Access practical utilities that improve script quality and language accuracy before text-to-speech generation.
tools.characterCounterDescription: Measure characters, words, sentences, and structure before generating voice output.
tools.languageDetectorDescription: Detect probable language and accent to reduce voice-selection mistakes.
tools.howToChooseTitle: How to pick the right tool
howToChooseItems: Use Character Counter when you are editing pacing, length, or script blocks.
Use Language Detector when locale certainty matters before synthesis.
Apply both tools before generating MP3 for client-facing assets.
whenToUseItems: Before large batch generation where errors are expensive.
Before multilingual releases with regional accent differences.
When script quality control is part of your publishing checklist.
tools.navigationDescription: Move from tool validation to full generation once your script and locale are stable.
